subject: Geography
title: Ptolemy's World Map
description: A fast breakdown of Ptolemy's World Map for builders.

hook.text: Ptolemy's 'Geography,' written in 150 CE, guided mapmakers for 1,400 years.

buildup.text: He introduced latitude and longitude, a grid system for locating any point on Earth.

discovery.text: His maps showed Europe, Africa, and Asia—everything the Roman world knew.

twist.text: Ptolemy made the Indian Ocean a landlocked sea and shrank Earth's size by 30%.

climax.text: Those errors persisted for over a millennium because no one could check his work.

punchline.text: The most influential map in history was beautifully wrong.

quiz.question: What coordinate system did Ptolemy introduce?
